seoTitle: How to Export the General Ledger to Excel in QBO
metaDescription: Find out how to export your General Ledger from QuickBooks Online to Excel so you can analyze transactions or share financial data outside the platform.
intro: Sometimes you need your General Ledger in spreadsheet form for deeper analysis or to share with an accountant. This guide walks through exporting the General Ledger report from QuickBooks Online to Excel step by step.

steps.heading: Open the General Ledger report
steps.body: Go to Reports and search for the General Ledger report from your QuickBooks Online account.

steps.heading: Set the date range and filters
steps.body: Choose the period and accounts you want included before exporting so the file only contains relevant data.

steps.heading: Export to Excel
steps.body: Use the export option on the report screen to download the ledger as an Excel file.

steps.heading: Open and review the file
steps.body: Open the downloaded spreadsheet to confirm the transactions and formatting came through as expected.

faq.question: Can I export just one account's ledger?
faq.answer: Yes, you can filter the General Ledger report to a specific account before exporting if you only need that data.

faq.question: Does the export include transaction details?
faq.answer: Yes, the General Ledger export typically includes transaction dates, descriptions, and amounts for each account.

faq.question: What file formats are available besides Excel?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online usually also offers a PDF export option alongside the Excel format.

faq.question: Will formulas or formatting carry over to Excel?
faq.answer: The export brings over the report data as static values, so you may need to add your own formulas once it's in Excel.
